arguments.query: clickup get task task details comments attachments checklists subtasks views dependencies time in status

tools.name: mcp__clickup
tools.description: Tools in the mcp__clickup namespace.

tools.name: clickup_create_comment
tools.description: Create a comment or threaded reply on a task, list, or view. Supports Markdown (headings, bold, code blocks, tables). Use entity_type + entity_id for the target entity. Provide reply_to_id for a threaded reply.

assignee.description: User ID to assign the comment to. Use clickup_resolve_assignees to convert email, username, or "me" to user ID if needed.

entity_id.description: ID of the entity to comment on.

entity_type.description: Entity type to comment on. Use with entity_id.

notify_all.description: Whether to notify all assignees. Default is false.

reply_to_id.description: ID of a parent comment to reply to. When provided, the comment is posted as a threaded reply instead of a top-level comment.

tools.name: clickup_get_task_comments
tools.description: Get task comments with reply_count per comment. Use clickup_get_threaded_comments for replies when reply_count > 0. Supports pagination via start/start_id.

start.description: Timestamp (in milliseconds) to start retrieving comments from. Used for pagination.

start_id.description: Comment ID to start from. Used together with start for pagination.

tools.name: clickup_get_bulk_tasks_time_in_status
tools.description: Get the time multiple tasks have spent in each status (bulk operation, up to 100 tasks). Returns a map of task IDs to their status history and current status time data. Requires the "Total time in Status" ClickApp to be enabled in the workspace.

task_ids.description: Array of task IDs to get time in status for (1-100 tasks). Works with both regular task IDs and custom IDs (like 'DEV-1234').

tools.name: clickup_filter_tasks
tools.description: Retrieve tasks with combined filters (tags, lists, folders, spaces, statuses, assignees, due date range, completion date range). Multiple values within a filter use OR logic; across filters, AND logic applies. Assignees must be numeric user IDs — use clickup_resolve_assignees to convert names/emails/"me". For a single task by ID, use clickup_get_task.

assignees.description: Filter by assignee user IDs. Multiple IDs use OR logic. Use clickup_resolve_assignees to convert names/emails/"me" first.

date_closed_from.description: Filter tasks completed on or after. Format: YYYY-MM-DD

date_closed_to.description: Filter tasks completed on or before. Format: YYYY-MM-DD

due_date_from.description: Filter tasks with due date on or after. Format: YYYY-MM-DD

due_date_to.description: Filter tasks with due date on or before. Format: YYYY-MM-DD

folder_ids.description: Filter by Folder IDs. Multiple IDs use OR logic (matches tasks in ANY of the specified folders).

list_ids.description: Filter by List IDs. Multiple IDs use OR logic (matches tasks in ANY of the specified lists).

space_ids.description: Filter by Space IDs. Multiple IDs use OR logic (matches tasks in ANY of the specified spaces).

statuses.description: Filter by task status names. Multiple statuses use OR logic (matches tasks with ANY of the specified statuses).

tags.description: Filter by tag names. Multiple tags use OR logic (matches tasks with ANY of the specified tags).

tools.name: clickup_get_task_time_in_status
tools.description: Get the time a task has spent in each status. Returns the current status with elapsed time and the full status history with time spent in each status. Requires the "Total time in Status" ClickApp to be enabled in the workspace.

task_id.description: ID of task. Works with both regular task IDs and custom IDs (like 'DEV-1234'). Use clickup_search to find task ID by name if needed.

tools.name: clickup_get_time_entries
tools.description: Get time entries with optional filtering by task, date range, assignee, and billable status. Pass task_id to scope to a single task, or omit for workspace-wide results. IMPORTANT: without assignee, only the authenticated user's entries are returned — pass 'any' to get all users' entries, or specific user IDs (comma-separated) for targeted queries.

assignee.description: Filter by assignee user IDs. Pass numeric user IDs (e.g. ['123', '456']) or include 'any' to get ALL users' entries. IMPORTANT: when omitted, the API returns only the authenticated user's entries. Use get_workspace_members to look up user IDs.

end_date.description: End date in YYYY-MM-DD format or date-time in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format

is_billable.description: Filter by billable status. Set to true for only billable entries, false for non-billable. Omit to get all entries.

start_date.description: Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format or date-time in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format

task_id.description: Task ID to scope entries to (supports custom IDs like 'DEV-1234'). Omit to query workspace-wide.

tools.name: clickup_search
tools.description: Search across all workspace content (tasks, docs, dashboards, attachments, whiteboards, chats, forms). Supports filtering by assignees, creators, status, location, asset types, and date ranges. Date filters use YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format (e.g., '2025-01-01' or '2025-01-01 09:00') in your timezone. Returns paginated results with hierarchy context.

parameters.description: Input parameters for searching ClickUp workspace content

count.description: Maximum number of results to return per page (for pagination)

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from previous response. Use to fetch next page of results

filters.description: Filters to refine search results by various criteria

asset_types.description: Filter by asset types (task, doc, whiteboard, dashboard, attachment, or chat)

items.description: Type of ClickUp asset to search for

assignees.description: Numeric user IDs. Use clickup_resolve_assignees to convert names/emails/"me".

created_date_from.description: Filter items created on or after this date. Format: YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM (e.g., "2025-01-01" or "2025-01-01 09:00")

created_date_to.description: Filter items created on or before this date. Format: YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM (e.g., "2025-12-31" or "2025-12-31 23:59")

creators.description: Numeric user IDs. Use clickup_resolve_assignees to convert names/emails/"me".

due_date_from.description: Filter items with due date on or after this date. Format: YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM (e.g., "2025-01-01" or "2025-01-01 09:00")

due_date_to.description: Filter items with due date on or before this date. Format: YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM (e.g., "2025-12-31" or "2025-12-31 23:59")

location.description: Location filters for hierarchical organization (Spaces, Folders, Lists)

categories.description: Filter by Folder IDs. Returns items from these Folders

projects.description: Filter by Space IDs. Returns items from these Spaces

subcategories.description: Filter by List IDs. Returns items from these Lists

task_statuses.description: Filter tasks by their status (unstarted, active, done, closed, or archived)

keywords.description: Search query string. Use specific keywords to find items

sort.description: Sort criteria for results. Can specify multiple sort fields in priority order

direction.description: Sort order (asc for ascending, desc for descending)

field.description: The field to sort by (created_at or updated_at)

tools.name: clickup_remove_task_dependency
tools.description: Remove a dependency between two tasks.

depends_on.description: ID of the task that was in the dependency relationship. Works with both regular task IDs and custom IDs (like 'DEV-1234').

task_id.description: ID of the task to remove the dependency from. Works with both regular task IDs and custom IDs (like 'DEV-1234'). Use clickup_search to find task ID by name if needed.

type.description: Type of dependency to remove: 'waiting_on' or 'blocking'.

tools.name: clickup_add_task_dependency
tools.description: Set a dependency between two tasks. Use 'waiting_on' when task_id cannot start until depends_on is done, or 'blocking' when task_id is blocking depends_on.

depends_on.description: ID of the task that task_id depends on or is blocking. Works with regular and custom IDs.

task_id.description: ID of the task to set the dependency on. Works with regular and custom IDs (like 'DEV-1234'). Use clickup_search to find task ID by name if needed.

type.description: Type of dependency to add: 'waiting_on' or 'blocking'.

tools.name: clickup_attach_task_file
tools.description: Attach file to task. Requires task_id. File sources: 1) base64 + filename, 2) URL (http/https).

auth_header.description: Authorization header to use when downloading from the web URL.

file_data.description: Base64-encoded content of the file (without the data URL prefix).

file_name.description: Name of the file to be attached (include the extension). Required when using file_data.

file_url.description: URL to download the file from (must start with http:// or https://).

tools.name: clickup_get_threaded_comments
tools.description: Get threaded replies for a comment by comment_id. Use clickup_get_task_comments first to find comments with reply_count > 0.

comment_id.description: ID of the parent comment to get threaded replies for.

tools.name: clickup_start_time_tracking
tools.description: Start time tracking on a task. Supports description, billable status, and tags. Only one timer can be running at a time. For best results, omit extra parameters unless specifically needed.

billable.description: Whether this time is billable. Default is workspace setting.

description.description: Description for the time entry. Keep short and simple, or omit for best compatibility.

tags.description: Array of tag names to assign to the time entry.

tools.name: clickup_get_list
tools.description: Get list details by list_id or list_name. Returns id, name, content, space info, and configured statuses. Use to resolve list names to IDs.

list_id.description: ID of the list to retrieve.

list_name.description: Name of the list to retrieve. The tool will search for a list with this name.

tools.name: clickup_add_time_entry
tools.description: Add a manual time entry to a task. You can provide either (start + duration) OR (start + end). The tool will calculate missing values. Requires task_id, start time, and either duration or end time. Supports description, billable flag, and tags.

billable.description: Whether this time is billable. Default is workspace setting.

description.description: Description for the time entry. Keep short and simple, or omit for best compatibility.

duration.description: Duration of the time entry. Format as 'Xh Ym' (e.g., '1h 30m') or just minutes (e.g., '90m'). Either duration or end_time is required.

end_time.description: End time in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format (e.g., '2025-01-15 11:00'). Time is required for time tracking entries.

start.description: Start time in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format (e.g., '2025-01-15 09:30'). Time is required for time tracking entries.

tags.description: Array of tag names to assign to the time entry.

tools.name: clickup_get_custom_fields
tools.description: Get custom field definitions at any hierarchy level (list, folder, space, or workspace). Returns field IDs, types, and options for dropdowns/labels. Use this to discover available custom fields before setting values on tasks. Multiple scopes can be queried in a single call.

folder_id.description: Folder ID. Returns custom fields defined on this folder.

include_workspace.description: If true, returns workspace-level custom fields.

list_id.description: List ID. Returns custom fields defined on this list.

space_id.description: Space ID. Returns custom fields defined on this space.

tools.name: clickup_create_task
tools.description: Create a task in a ClickUp list. Requires name and list_id — always ask the user which list. Supports assignees (user IDs, emails, usernames, or "me") and task_type by name.

assignees.description: Array of assignee user IDs. Use clickup_resolve_assignees to convert emails, usernames, or "me" to user IDs if needed.

check_required_custom_fields.description: Flag to check if all required custom fields are set before saving the task.

custom_fields.description: Array of custom field values to set on the task. Each object must have an 'id' and 'value' property.

value.description: Value for the custom field. Pass as a string — the format depends on the field type: text/url/email: the value directly. phone: must be a valid phone number in international format (e.g. '+1 234 567 8901'). dropdown: the option UUID. number/money/rating: the number as a string (e.g. '42'). date: a date string in YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format (auto-converted to timestamp). checkbox/button: 'true' or 'false'. labels: JSON array of UUIDs (e.g. '["uuid1","uuid2"]'). relationships/people/files: JSON with add/rem arrays (e.g. '{"add":["id1"],"rem":["id2"]}'). progress: JSON with current value (e.g. '{"current":50}'). location: JSON with location object (e.g. '{"location":{"lat":-28,"lng":153},"formatted_address":"..."}').

due_date.description: Due date in YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format

list_id.description: List ID. Use clickup_get_list to resolve names.

name.description: Task name. Ask the user what they want to name the task.

parent.description: Parent task ID to create as subtask.

start_date.description: Start date in YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format

status.description: Override default status. Omit to use list defaults.

tags.description: Tag names (must already exist in the space).

task_type.description: Name of the task type (e.g., 'Bug', 'Feature', 'Milestone'). The type must exist in the workspace. If not specified, the default task type will be used.

tools.name: clickup_get_task
tools.description: Retrieve a ClickUp task by ID (supports custom IDs like 'DEV-1234'). Returns a compact summary by default — core fields are always included, large sections appear as counts only (e.g. custom_fields_count: 3). Use include to fetch full data for specific sections: include: ["custom_fields", "description"]. Set expand_statuses=true to list valid statuses for update_task.

expand_statuses.description: When true, returns the full set of statuses configured on the task's list (including any inherited from the parent folder or space) under `available_statuses`. Use to discover which status values can be assigned via update_task. Off by default to keep responses lean.

include.description: Sections to return in full. Without this, large sections appear as counts (e.g. custom_fields_count: 3). Available: custom_fields (field values), checklists (items + completion), dependencies (blocking/waiting-on relationships), linked_tasks (linked task IDs), watchers (watching users), subtasks (triggers subtask fetch from API), description (full text — summary truncates at 10k chars).

tools.name: clickup_update_task
tools.description: Update task properties. Requires task_id and at least one field to change. Supports assignees (user IDs, emails, usernames, or "me"), custom fields as [{id, value}], and task_type by name (or 'none' to reset).

assignees.description: Array of assignee user IDs. Use clickup_resolve_assignees to convert emails, usernames, or "me" to user IDs if needed.

custom_fields.description: Array of custom field values to set on the task. Each object must have an 'id' and 'value' property.

value.description: Value for the custom field. Pass as a string — the format depends on the field type: text/url/email: the value directly. phone: must be a valid phone number in international format (e.g. '+1 234 567 8901'). dropdown: the option UUID. number/money/rating: the number as a string (e.g. '42'). date: a date string in YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format (auto-converted to timestamp). checkbox/button: 'true' or 'false'. labels: JSON array of UUIDs (e.g. '["uuid1","uuid2"]'). relationships/people/files: JSON with add/rem arrays (e.g. '{"add":["id1"],"rem":["id2"]}'). progress: JSON with current value (e.g. '{"current":50}'). location: JSON with location object (e.g. '{"location":{"lat":-28,"lng":153},"formatted_address":"..."}').

due_date.description: YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format. Pass 'none' to clear. Omit to leave unchanged.

priority.description: Set priority, or 'none' to clear. Omit to leave unchanged.

start_date.description: YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format. Pass 'none' to clear. Omit to leave unchanged.

status.description: New status (must be valid for the task's list).

task_type.description: To change the task type, pass the type name as a string (e.g., 'Bug', 'Feature', 'Milestone'). The type must exist in the workspace. To revert to the default 'Task' type, pass 'none'. Omit entirely to leave unchanged.

tools.name: clickup_merge_tasks
tools.description: Merge one or more source tasks into a target task. The target task survives and absorbs content from the source tasks, which are consumed. Destination field values take precedence on conflicts. Works with both regular task IDs and custom IDs (like 'DEV-1234').

source_task_ids.description: Array of task IDs to merge into the target task. These tasks will be consumed/deleted after merging. Works with both regular task IDs and custom IDs (like 'DEV-1234').

task_id.description: ID of the target/destination task that will survive the merge. Source tasks will be merged into this task. Works with both regular task IDs and custom IDs (like 'DEV-1234').

tools.name: mcp__trigger
tools.description:
Trigger.dev MCP server to automate your Trigger.dev projects and answer questions about Trigger.dev by searching the docs. 
If you need help setting up Trigger.dev in your project please refer to https://trigger.dev/docs/manual-setup. 
If the user asks for help with adding Trigger.dev to their project, please refer to https://trigger.dev/docs/manual-setup.

tools.name: get_current_worker
tools.description: Get the current worker for the project, including version and registered task slugs. Use get_task_schema to get the payload schema for a specific task.

branch.description: The branch to get tasks for, only used for preview environments

configPath.description: The path to the trigger.config.ts file. Only used when the trigger.config.ts file is not at the root dir (like in a monorepo setup). If not provided, we will try to find the config file in the current working directory

environment.description: The environment to get tasks for

projectRef.description: The trigger.dev project ref, starts with proj_. We will attempt to automatically detect the project ref if running inside a directory that includes a trigger.config.ts file, or if you pass the --project-ref option to the MCP server.

arguments.query: clickup views list views get view folder list space board kanban dashboard

tools.name: mcp__clickup
tools.description: Tools in the mcp__clickup namespace.

tools.name: clickup_create_comment
tools.description: Create a comment or threaded reply on a task, list, or view. Supports Markdown (headings, bold, code blocks, tables). Use entity_type + entity_id for the target entity. Provide reply_to_id for a threaded reply.

assignee.description: User ID to assign the comment to. Use clickup_resolve_assignees to convert email, username, or "me" to user ID if needed.

entity_id.description: ID of the entity to comment on.

entity_type.description: Entity type to comment on. Use with entity_id.

notify_all.description: Whether to notify all assignees. Default is false.

reply_to_id.description: ID of a parent comment to reply to. When provided, the comment is posted as a threaded reply instead of a top-level comment.

tools.name: clickup_get_workspace_hierarchy
tools.description: Get workspace hierarchy (spaces, folders, lists) with pagination and depth control. Use only when you need the workspace structure — most tools resolve names automatically.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from previous response. Use to fetch next page of spaces

limit.description: Maximum number of spaces to return per page (default: 10, max: 50)

max_depth.description: Maximum depth of hierarchy to return: 0=spaces only (default), 1=spaces+folders, 2=spaces+folders+lists. Use higher depths only when the user asks about folders or lists

space_ids.description: Filter to return only specific spaces by ID.

tools.name: clickup_create_list_in_folder
tools.description: Create a list in a ClickUp folder. Requires folder_id and list name. Supports content and status. If you need to get a folder ID from a folder name, use clickup_get_folder first.

content.description: Description or content of the list.

folder_id.description: ID of the folder to create the list in.

status.description: Status of the list (uses folder default if not specified).

tools.name: clickup_get_custom_fields
tools.description: Get custom field definitions at any hierarchy level (list, folder, space, or workspace). Returns field IDs, types, and options for dropdowns/labels. Use this to discover available custom fields before setting values on tasks. Multiple scopes can be queried in a single call.

folder_id.description: Folder ID. Returns custom fields defined on this folder.

include_workspace.description: If true, returns workspace-level custom fields.

list_id.description: List ID. Returns custom fields defined on this list.

space_id.description: Space ID. Returns custom fields defined on this space.

tools.name: clickup_update_folder
tools.description: Update a ClickUp folder. Requires folder_id + at least one update field (name/override_statuses). Only specified fields updated. Changes apply to all lists in folder. If you need to get a folder ID from a folder name, use clickup_get_folder first.

folder_id.description: ID of the folder to update.

override_statuses.description: Whether to override space statuses with folder-specific statuses.

tools.name: clickup_create_folder
tools.description: Create folder in ClickUp space. Use space_id (preferred) or space_name + folder name. Supports override_statuses for folder-specific statuses. Use clickup_create_list_in_folder to add lists after creation.

override_statuses.description: Whether to override space statuses with folder-specific statuses.

space_id.description: ID of the space to create the folder in (preferred). Provide this instead of space_name if you already have it.

space_name.description: Name of the space to create the folder in. Use this when space_id is not available.

tools.name: clickup_get_folder
tools.description: Get folder details by folder_id or folder_name (+ space info). Use to resolve folder names to IDs.

folder_id.description: ID of the folder to retrieve.

folder_name.description: Name of the folder to retrieve. When using this, you must also provide space_id or space_name.

space_id.description: ID of the space containing the folder (required with folder_name).

space_name.description: Name of the space containing the folder (required with folder_name).

tools.name: clickup_create_list
tools.description: Create a list in a ClickUp space. Requires name and space_name or space_id. For lists in folders, use clickup_create_list_in_folder.

assignee.description: User ID to assign the list to. Use clickup_resolve_assignees to convert email, username, or "me" to user ID if needed.

content.description: Description or content of the list.

due_date.description: Due date in YYYY-MM-DD format or date-time in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format

space_id.description: ID of the space to create the list in. Provide this instead of space_name if you already have the ID.

space_name.description: Name of the space to create the list in. Alternative to space_id; one of them must be provided.

tools.name: clickup_create_document
tools.description: Create a document in a ClickUp space, folder, or list. Requires name, parent info, visibility and create_page flag.

create_page.description: Whether to create an initial blank page

name.description: Name and Title of the document

id.description: ID of the parent container (space, folder, or list)

tools.name: clickup_filter_tasks
tools.description: Retrieve tasks with combined filters (tags, lists, folders, spaces, statuses, assignees, due date range, completion date range). Multiple values within a filter use OR logic; across filters, AND logic applies. Assignees must be numeric user IDs — use clickup_resolve_assignees to convert names/emails/"me". For a single task by ID, use clickup_get_task.

assignees.description: Filter by assignee user IDs. Multiple IDs use OR logic. Use clickup_resolve_assignees to convert names/emails/"me" first.

date_closed_from.description: Filter tasks completed on or after. Format: YYYY-MM-DD

date_closed_to.description: Filter tasks completed on or before. Format: YYYY-MM-DD

due_date_from.description: Filter tasks with due date on or after. Format: YYYY-MM-DD

due_date_to.description: Filter tasks with due date on or before. Format: YYYY-MM-DD

folder_ids.description: Filter by Folder IDs. Multiple IDs use OR logic (matches tasks in ANY of the specified folders).

list_ids.description: Filter by List IDs. Multiple IDs use OR logic (matches tasks in ANY of the specified lists).

space_ids.description: Filter by Space IDs. Multiple IDs use OR logic (matches tasks in ANY of the specified spaces).

statuses.description: Filter by task status names. Multiple statuses use OR logic (matches tasks with ANY of the specified statuses).

tags.description: Filter by tag names. Multiple tags use OR logic (matches tasks with ANY of the specified tags).

tools.name: clickup_get_list
tools.description: Get list details by list_id or list_name. Returns id, name, content, space info, and configured statuses. Use to resolve list names to IDs.

list_id.description: ID of the list to retrieve.

list_name.description: Name of the list to retrieve. The tool will search for a list with this name.

tools.name: clickup_update_list
tools.description: Update a ClickUp list. Requires list_id + at least one update field (name/content/status). Only specified fields updated. If you need to get a list ID from a list name, use clickup_get_list first.

content.description: New description or content for the list.

list_id.description: ID of the list to update.

tools.name: clickup_remove_task_from_list
tools.description: Remove a task from an additional list (cannot remove from home list). Requires the Tasks in Multiple Lists ClickApp to be enabled.

list_id.description: ID of the additional list to remove the task from. Use clickup_get_list to find the list ID from a list name if needed.

task_id.description: ID of the task to remove from the additional list. Works with regular and custom IDs (like 'DEV-1234'). Note: a task cannot be removed from its home list.

tools.name: clickup_add_task_to_list
tools.description: Add a task to an additional list (keeps current home list). Requires the Tasks in Multiple Lists ClickApp to be enabled.

list_id.description: ID of the additional list to add the task to. The task remains in its original list. Use clickup_get_list to find the list ID from a list name if needed.

task_id.description: ID of the task to add. Works with regular and custom IDs (like 'DEV-1234'). Use clickup_search to find task ID by name if needed.

tools.name: clickup_create_task
tools.description: Create a task in a ClickUp list. Requires name and list_id — always ask the user which list. Supports assignees (user IDs, emails, usernames, or "me") and task_type by name.

assignees.description: Array of assignee user IDs. Use clickup_resolve_assignees to convert emails, usernames, or "me" to user IDs if needed.

check_required_custom_fields.description: Flag to check if all required custom fields are set before saving the task.

custom_fields.description: Array of custom field values to set on the task. Each object must have an 'id' and 'value' property.

value.description: Value for the custom field. Pass as a string — the format depends on the field type: text/url/email: the value directly. phone: must be a valid phone number in international format (e.g. '+1 234 567 8901'). dropdown: the option UUID. number/money/rating: the number as a string (e.g. '42'). date: a date string in YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format (auto-converted to timestamp). checkbox/button: 'true' or 'false'. labels: JSON array of UUIDs (e.g. '["uuid1","uuid2"]'). relationships/people/files: JSON with add/rem arrays (e.g. '{"add":["id1"],"rem":["id2"]}'). progress: JSON with current value (e.g. '{"current":50}'). location: JSON with location object (e.g. '{"location":{"lat":-28,"lng":153},"formatted_address":"..."}').

due_date.description: Due date in YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format

list_id.description: List ID. Use clickup_get_list to resolve names.

name.description: Task name. Ask the user what they want to name the task.

parent.description: Parent task ID to create as subtask.

start_date.description: Start date in YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format

status.description: Override default status. Omit to use list defaults.

tags.description: Tag names (must already exist in the space).

task_type.description: Name of the task type (e.g., 'Bug', 'Feature', 'Milestone'). The type must exist in the workspace. If not specified, the default task type will be used.

tools.name: clickup_get_workspace_members
tools.description: List all members in the workspace. Most tools resolve assignees automatically — use only when you need the full member list.

tools.name: mcp__1password
tools.description: Tools in the mcp__1password namespace.

tools.name: item_archive
tools.description: Archive an item in a 1Password vault. The item is moved to the archive and hidden from regular views, rather than being permanently deleted.

tools.name: mcp__trigger
tools.description:
Trigger.dev MCP server to automate your Trigger.dev projects and answer questions about Trigger.dev by searching the docs. 
If you need help setting up Trigger.dev in your project please refer to https://trigger.dev/docs/manual-setup. 
If the user asks for help with adding Trigger.dev to their project, please refer to https://trigger.dev/docs/manual-setup.

tools.name: dev_server_status
tools.description: Check the status of the dev server and view recent output. Shows whether it is stopped, starting, ready, or has errors, along with recent log lines.

lines.description: Number of recent log lines to return. Defaults to 50.

tools.name: list_dashboards
tools.description: List available built-in dashboards with their widgets. Each dashboard contains pre-built queries for common metrics like run success rates, costs, LLM usage, and more. Use run_dashboard to execute a dashboard's queries.

branch.description: The branch to get tasks for, only used for preview environments

configPath.description: The path to the trigger.config.ts file. Only used when the trigger.config.ts file is not at the root dir (like in a monorepo setup). If not provided, we will try to find the config file in the current working directory

environment.description: The environment to get tasks for

projectRef.description: The trigger.dev project ref, starts with proj_. We will attempt to automatically detect the project ref if running inside a directory that includes a trigger.config.ts file, or if you pass the --project-ref option to the MCP server.

tools.name: mcp__playwright
tools.description: Tools in the mcp__playwright namespace.

tools.name: browser_network_requests
tools.description: Returns a numbered list of network requests since loading the page. Use browser_network_request with the number to get full details.

filename.description: Filename to save the network requests to. If not provided, requests are returned as text.

filter.description: Only return requests whose URL matches this regexp (e.g. "/api/.*user").

static.description: Whether to include successful static resources like images, fonts, scripts, etc. Defaults to false.

1. Materials: cotton rope with embroidered applique (see below for available fabrics for applique)
2. Size: 7.5" high, 10.5" top diameter, 8" bottom diameter
3. Packaging: hangtag that is swift-tagged to item, sewn-in label for legal line
4. Production limitations: embroidery can’t exceed 30cm from the top of basket to bottom of embroidery because of limit of embroidery machine. Felt (nonwoven), woven fabric, PU are most common fabrics used for the applique part of this item.
5. Demographic: kids/juvenile
6. Season: NON seasonal
7. Photos of item are attached in this card

Save all costing sheets here: R:\\Decor\\Character Licensed\\\_\_\_\_New Structure\\Product Ideas\\Product Formats\\2024-06\_Cotton rope basket with embroidered applique

DESIGNS: **_Sarbani asked new freelancer Mauricio for new designs of this product type 062024_**
Please create 4 new designs of this product type, a cotton rope basket with embroidered applique. Please make 1 design for each of these properties:
1.     1. Angel (from Lilo & Stitch property)
    2. Winnie the Pooh
    3. Strawberry Shortcake
    4. Star Wars (Grogu) - for this one you can create attached plush ears like the example Stitch basket
    5. Care Bears - Grumpy Bear
    6. ~~Care Bears - Cheer Bear~~
    7. NBX Jack Skellington
    8. Harry Potter (Hedwig)
    9. Snoopy (by himself)
    10. Spongebob Squarepants (make the bottom rows of rope brown to match his pants)

Production limitations: embroidery can’t exceed 30cm from the top of basket to bottom of embroidery because of limit of embroidery machine. Felt (nonwoven), woven fabric, PU are most common fabrics used for the applique part of this item.

structuredContent.name: Cotton rope basket with embroidered applique - Stitch & Spiderman embroidery
structuredContent.markdown_description:
We need to create costing tech packs for the attached rope baskets with embroidered applique.
1. Materials: cotton rope with embroidered applique (see below for available fabrics for applique)
2. Size: 7.5" high, 10.5" top diameter, 8" bottom diameter
3. Packaging: hangtag that is swift-tagged to item, sewn-in label for legal line
4. Production limitations: embroidery can’t exceed 30cm from the top of basket to bottom of embroidery because of limit of embroidery machine. Felt (nonwoven), woven fabric, PU are most common fabrics used for the applique part of this item.
5. Demographic: kids/juvenile
6. Season: NON seasonal
7. Photos of item are attached in this card

Save all costing sheets here: R:\\Decor\\Character Licensed\\\_\_\_\_New Structure\\Product Ideas\\Product Formats\\2024-06\_Cotton rope basket with embroidered applique

DESIGNS: **_Sarbani asked new freelancer Mauricio for new designs of this product type 062024_**
Please create 4 new designs of this product type, a cotton rope basket with embroidered applique. Please make 1 design for each of these properties:
1.     1. Angel (from Lilo & Stitch property)
    2. Winnie the Pooh
    3. Strawberry Shortcake
    4. Star Wars (Grogu) - for this one you can create attached plush ears like the example Stitch basket
    5. Care Bears - Grumpy Bear
    6. ~~Care Bears - Cheer Bear~~
    7. NBX Jack Skellington
    8. Harry Potter (Hedwig)
    9. Snoopy (by himself)
    10. Spongebob Squarepants (make the bottom rows of rope brown to match his pants)

Production limitations: embroidery can’t exceed 30cm from the top of basket to bottom of embroidery because of limit of embroidery machine. Felt (nonwoven), woven fabric, PU are most common fabrics used for the applique part of this item.

Save all designs here: R:\\Decor\\Character Licensed\\\_\_\_\_New Structure\\Product Ideas\\Product Formats\\2024-06\_Cotton rope basket with embroidered applique

{"id":"86892rzhm","custom_id":null,"custom_item_id":0,"name":"Star Wars Hexagon Bin - HomeGoods","markdown_description":""Tie Fighter in the middle of the bottom row of the last page and as it is it looks like it’s made of MDF, like furniture.

We wouldn’t do it that way. We would do it like we did the half-moon hampers: steel wire hexagons for each side, with 4 horizontal steel wires connecting the 2 hexagons, with everything wrapped in printed oxford bonded to EVA foam. Can you mock it up that way with callouts so we can send back to the factories?"

Save all files here: R:\\Decor\\Character Licensed\\\_\_\_\_New Structure\\In Development\\Customer Adopted\\HomeGoods Storage FH24\\\_star wars storage bin

We always need information on each new card for the new product:
*   Product Type
*   Size
*   Material
*   Production Limitations: Insights from the factory about colors and materials.
*   Product Photos
*   Deadline: Time Frame
*   Level of Priority

Once this information is received, we can send this over to Costing Sheet elaboration, to be assigned to a designer who will make the costing sheet.","text_content":""Tie Fighter in the middle of the bottom row of the last page and as it is it looks like it’s made of MDF, like furniture.

We wouldn’t do it that way. We would do it like we did the half-moon hampers: steel wire hexagons for each side, with 4 horizontal steel wires connecting the 2 hexagons, with everything wrapped in printed oxford bonded to EVA foam. Can you mock it up that way with callouts so we can send back to the factories?"

Save all files here: R:\Decor\Character Licensed\____New Structure\In Development\Customer Adopted\HomeGoods Storage FH24\_star wars storage bin

We always need information on each new card for the new product:

Size
Material

structuredContent.name: Star Wars Hexagon Bin - HomeGoods
structuredContent.markdown_description:
"Tie Fighter in the middle of the bottom row of the last page and as it is it looks like it’s made of MDF, like furniture.

We wouldn’t do it that way. We would do it like we did the half-moon hampers: steel wire hexagons for each side, with 4 horizontal steel wires connecting the 2 hexagons, with everything wrapped in printed oxford bonded to EVA foam. Can you mock it up that way with callouts so we can send back to the factories?"

Save all files here: R:\\Decor\\Character Licensed\\\_\_\_\_New Structure\\In Development\\Customer Adopted\\HomeGoods Storage FH24\\\_star wars storage bin

We always need information on each new card for the new product:
*   Product Type
*   Size
*   Material
*   Production Limitations: Insights from the factory about colors and materials.
*   Product Photos
*   Deadline: Time Frame
*   Level of Priority

Once this information is received, we can send this over to Costing Sheet elaboration, to be assigned to a designer who will make the costing sheet.

Can you mock it up that way with callouts so we can send back to the factories?"

Size
Material
Production Limitations: Insights from the factory about colors and materials.

Once this information is received, we can send this over to Costing Sheet elaboration, to be assigned to a designer who will make the costing sheet.
